---
title: "Motivation 4 — COMBSS in one page"
subtitle: "Where it sits between lasso and MIO"
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
*Draft outline — content to come.*
:::

## The bridge

| | **Lasso** | **MIO** | **COMBSS** |
|---|---|---|---|
| Variable type | $\beta \in \mathbb{R}^p$ | $z \in \{0,1\}^p$ | $t \in [0,1]^p$ |
| Sparsity by | $\ell_1$ penalty | hard $\ell_0$ + Big-$M$ | continuous relaxation on the support indicator |
| Selection size | indexed by $\lambda$ | $k$ via constraint | $k$ via polytope |
| Scales to $p \gg n$ | yes | barely | yes |
| Biased coefficients | yes | no | no (envelope) |

## In one sentence

COMBSS relaxes the discrete support indicator $z \in \{0,1\}^p$ to a continuous $t \in [0,1]^p$ on the cardinality polytope, fits a ridge GLM in the relaxed space, and runs a Frank-Wolfe homotopy that drives $t$ to a vertex of the polytope — recovering an integer subset of size exactly $k$.

## Preview of the rest of the talk

- **Methodology** — how the relaxation works, the Frank-Wolfe step, the curvature homotopy.
- **Demos** — five runnable demos showing the method in action.

---

[← MIO](03-mio.qmd) · [Methodology → Boolean relaxation](../methodology/01-relaxation.qmd)
